The Bureau of Motor Vehicles will never send you a text message with information about a potential suspension or unpaid infractions. Do

click on links in any unsolicited texts. If you were scammed, contact your financial institution immediately. If you are unsure if a message you have received is genuine, contact the BMV at 207-624-9000.
ROAD READY PARENT SUPERVISED DRIVING PROGRAM:

Road Ready recently had to make an update that modernized their database structure. No one currently using the application has lost any data; it's a syncing issue. Parents of drivers who use the application to track driving hours should have received an email informing you how to resolve any issues. Please search your emails (including SPAM folder) and attempt to locate those instructions. If you cannot locate the instructions, please send an email to

Welcome to the Bureau of Motor Vehicles!
Established in 1905, the BMV works to protect all who use Maine’s roadways. We:
Retain Vehicle Registration and Title Records
BMV also provides funds for building and maintaining our highways. This is done through licensing, title, and registration fees.
BMV works to provide easily accessible services to all Mainers. For example, we accomplish this is through our online efforts.
Make an appointment at the BMV online
Schedule an appointment at a BMV branch office

Our Bureau of Motor Vehicles branch offices are currently experiencing exceptionally high customer volumes due to increased demand for Real ID credentials. While we continue to welcome walk-in customers, please be aware that wait times may exceed three hours during peak periods in some locations.

To help reduce your wait, we strongly encourage you to schedule an appointment in advance. Appointments are available at all branch locations and are released daily, so we recommend checking frequently if you don’t see immediate availability.

If you're upgrading to a Real ID, please plan to visit us approximately two months before your planned travel. This will help ensure you receive your credential in time. Be sure to review the list of required documents before your visit.
Before visiting a branch, we also recommend reviewing the many online services available, which may allow you to complete your transaction without coming into an office.
Thank you for your patience and understanding as we work to serve all customers as efficiently as possible.

! We’re here Monday – Friday 8:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m. at 207-624-9000.
A working group to evaluate potential regulations of the vehicle towing industry and the impact on state agencies and industries as established by the Maine Legislature in 2025.
Resolve, Chapter 77, will be held on the second and fourth Thursday of each month from 1:00 pm to 2:30 pm eastern time, beginning September 25, 2025, through February 5, 2026.
A working group to study the Safety and use of non-conforming vehicles on Maine’s roads and highways as established by the Maine Legislature in 2025
Resolve, Chapter 29 will be held on the 1
Monday of each month from 1:00 to 2:30 pm, beginning October 6, 2025, through February 6, 2026.

The Maine Bureau of Motor Vehicles, within the constitutional office of the Department of the Secretary of State, was established in 1905 out of concern for public safety following the invention, mass production, and use of automobiles. Since that time, the Bureau has served the residents of the State and all users of Maine highways by qualifying and licensing drivers and by maintaining records of driver history, vehicle ownership and vehicle registration. Through the collection of the licensing and registration fees associated with these services, the BMV also provides revenue to build and maintain Maine's highways.

Today, through the leadership of the Secretary of State, the Bureau offers a variety of online services in addition to its familiar branch office locations. Using online services, for example, Maine motorists may renew their driver's license or order a duplicate license, renew their vehicle registration, or check on the availability of personalized license plates. Many other online services are available to businesses, individual motorists and other customers.
Effective May 7, 2025, REAL ID is required for domestic flights and certain federal facilities. Learn more about the requirements.
Pine Tree Plate Distribution Began May 2025
